message:
feat(airtable): add bounded linked-record expansion to list_records and get_record

Introduce optional linked-record resolution that follows foreign-key
chains across Airtable tables without unbounded joins. Agents can now
request nested record expansion with configurable depth and record limits,
eliminating the common pattern of issuing N+1 sequential API calls to
reconstruct relational data.

Implementation details:
- LinkedExpansionConfig: controls which fields to expand, max traversal
  depth (1-3), and total resolved record budget (1-50)
- LinkedExpansionState: tracks remaining budget and visited (table,record)
  pairs to prevent infinite cycles in self-referential schemas
- expand_linked_records_for_list/get_record: recursive async expansion
  that respects per-invocation bounds and cycle detection
- parse_linked_expansion_config: validates field refs against schema,
  ensures only linked-record fields are requested
- validate_linked_projection: prevents requesting linked_field_refs that
  are excluded from the projected fields list
- build_field_metadata: emits per-field type metadata including
  read_only/computed markers and linked-table summaries
- collect_visible_fields_for_list/collect_record_field_names: helpers
  for determining which fields appear in the response

New API parameters on list_records and get_record:
  - expand_linked_records: boolean toggle
  - linked_field_refs: optional subset of fields to expand
  - linked_record_depth: max traversal depth (default 1, max 3)
  - linked_record_limit: max records resolved (default 25, max 50)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
